mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL's predictive power vanishes in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise > 1% MoM
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are negative in 70%+ of instances where both GDPC1 and INDPRO exceed 1% growth, or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 4%) indicates slack in the labor market, prompting fiscal stimulus and monetary accommodation that drives S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX underperforms by > 2% in 50%+ of high UNRATE periods, or unemployment fails to predict returns in 3-year rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances with PAYEMS > 150k, or the coefficient on PAYEMS in regression models falls below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST < 1400k) signals weak demand for residential construction, reducing related sector earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX outperforms by > 1.5% in 60%+ of low HOUST periods, or HOUST's predictive power drops below 0.08 in rolling IC tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS > 660B) combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 3%) indicates sustainable demand without price pressures, boosting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60d returns are negative in 55%+ of instances where RSAFS > 660B and CPIAUCSL < 3%, or interaction term significance drops below 0.1 in regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 101) signals supply-side disruptions, increasing input costs and reducing corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of INDPRO decline periods, or the coefficient on INDPRO in regressions falls below -0.12.
